author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix(reader): keep code blocks with hyperlinked identifiers in extraction

Readability's _cleanConditionally strips div wrappers whose link density
exceeds 0.2. On Quarto/downlit-rendered sites every function name inside
code links to its reference docs, so short code blocks were deleted from
reader mode and EPUB export (27 of 39 blocks on the reported page).
Skip conditional cleaning for any element containing a pre code block,
mirroring the existing inside-a-code-tag guard.

Fixes #288

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix: keyboard not opening in save-file and instapaper dialogs

AlertController sets FLAG_ALT_FOCUSABLE_IM on the dialog window when
canTextInput() finds no text editor in the custom view. A ComposeView
has not composed yet at that point, so dialogs whose text fields live
in Compose are wrongly excluded from IME targeting and the soft
keyboard can never appear (regression from the XML-to-Compose dialog
refactoring in e966166eb).

Clear the flag after show() so the IME targets the dialog window again.

Fixes #610

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(vertical-read): reliable toggle, line-snapped paging, CJK typography, reader settings

Enhances the CJK vertical (tategaki) reading mode across correctness,
typography, layout, and code health.

Toggle & navigation
- Fix vertical mode getting stuck on: exiting no longer relies on
  webView.reload() (a no-op after reader mode replaces the body via
  document.body.outerHTML). Tear down reader mode in place and, if the
  user was reading horizontally, re-enter the horizontal reader.
- Track how vertical was entered so exit returns to the right state
  (normal page vs. horizontal reader); keep isVerticalRead in lockstep
  with toggleReaderMode so the reader-settings re-entry preserves it.
- Snap vertical page turns to line boundaries: measure the rendered line
  advance (measure_line_advance.js) and page by an integer multiple of
  it, anchored from the document's right edge, so a column is never
  sliced at the screen edge. Fix jumpToBottom (was == jumpToTop).

Typography (process_text_nodes.js)
- Tate-chu-yoko via text-combine-upright: all instead of the brittle
  rotate(-90deg) + hand-tuned-margin spans.
- Zero glyph 025CB -> 03007 ideographic zero in kanji dates.
- Full-width punctuation only in CJK context (Latin prose, numbers, URLs
  keep half-width); add ; ( ) alongside , : ! ? % /.
- 3-letter all-caps acronyms (USA, CPU) stack upright instead of
  rotating sideways.

Reader layout settings in vertical mode
- Page margin and line spacing now apply (line-height sets inter-line
  spacing under writing-mode: vertical-rl); two-column stays horizontal
  only. Re-measure line advance after a spacing change so paging stays
  snapped.

Progressive enhancement (vertical_layout.css)
- text-spacing-trim, line-break: strict, and native vertical ruby (rt)
  styling. Confirmed <ruby> furigana survives Readability.

Code health
- Extract inline VERTICAL_LAYOUT_CSS to an asset; drop the never-used
  isVertical branch of injectMozReaderModeJs; remove dead
  isAllCapitalLetter and leftover console.log.

Test: test_server/vertical.html exercises dates, tate-chu-yoko, acronyms,
ordered lists, ruby, and CJK/Latin punctuation for regression checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(settings): consolidate reader mode options into the reader settings dialog

- Settings > Appearance: the "Padding for reader mode" value field is
  replaced by a "Reader mode settings" action item that opens the same
  dialog as long-pressing the reader mode icon (changes there apply on
  the next reader mode entry since settings has no live webview)
- Settings > Misc: "Reader Mode: Keep Extra Content" removed; the option
  now lives in the reader settings dialog as a switch. Toggling it while
  reader mode is active re-enters reader mode so the page is re-parsed
  with the new content scope immediately
- keep_extra_content string derived per locale from the old setting
  title (prefix stripped); the four now-unused settings strings removed
  from all 31 locale files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(reader): reader layout settings dialog on long-press of reader mode icon

Long-pressing the reader mode icon (toolbar or menu dialog) opens a new
settings dialog with live-applied page margin, line spacing, a two-column
landscape switch, and a shortcut to the reader font dialog.

- New "readerSettings" CSS slot recomputed by updateReaderSettingsStyle();
  selectors outrank readerview.css so slot order doesn't matter
- Two-column landscape flows the reader body into viewport-height columns
  (column-count:2, column-fill:auto); page turns jump one viewport-wide
  spread via scrollTo page anchoring, and the viewport meta pins
  initial/minimum scale so the wide column strip can't trigger zoom-out
- body margin reset to 0 in two-column mode: the 8px UA default made
  spreads tile 16px short and drift one clipped glyph per page
- Replaces the setPadding JS call; slot + meta fully restored on exit
- Strings localized in all 30 locales; test_server/article.html added
  for reader-mode verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
refactor(font): managed CSS slots — font/style changes apply without reload

Replace append-only anonymous <style> injection with ID-addressed slot
elements (update_css_slot.js): updating a slot replaces its CSS, empty
CSS removes it, and unchanged CSS skips the DOM write (no e-ink repaint).

Fixes several "font change does nothing" cases:
- switching away from CUSTOM font lost to the stale higher-specificity
  rule left in <head> (append-only styles never removed)
- Google-font @import rules were silently dropped whenever black-font
  CSS preceded them in the blob; font CSS is now built first
- reader font stuck after exiting reader mode; reader/vertical slots
  are cleared on exit and the main slot is recomputed
- background tabs kept their load-time font; styles re-apply on tab
  switch in TabManager.showAlbum
- readerCustomFontInfo setter checked fontType instead of readerFontType

Reverting (system default font, bold/black/white-background off) now
clears the slot instead of reloading the page. Custom font URLs are
versioned by a hash of the font file rather than a per-call counter, so
repeated updates hit the cache while a new font file forces a refetch.
Boldness slider now routes through updateCssStyle() instead of its own
never-cleared style injection, and respects per-site boldness overrides.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(eink-image): fast CSS-filter mode + pipeline fixes

Deep/Fast mode selector in the E-ink Image Optimization dialog:
- Deep (default): existing network-layer pipeline with dithering
- Fast: injects img { filter: brightness/contrast/saturate } scaled by
  strength via updateCssStyle — no decode/re-encode cost, also covers
  data:/blob:/JS-generated images; preview uses an equivalent
  ColorMatrix so the dialog stays faithful per mode
- mode labels translated in all locales

Deep pipeline fixes (from the algorithm investigation):
- bake EXIF orientation into pixels (re-encode strips EXIF, so camera
  photos rendered rotated)
- pass image/gif through untouched (was frozen to first frame, served
  as JPEG labeled image/gif)
- cap decode size near the screen's long edge via inSampleSize: faster
  decode/process/encode, no more 3x48MB peaks on 12MP photos
- serve original bytes when processing is skipped (tiny images,
  decode failure) instead of forcing a WebView re-fetch
- decode inMutable (drops one full-bitmap copy), Q12 fixed-point
  saturation, Semaphore(2) bounds concurrent processing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(settings): live preview dialog for E-ink image optimization

The E-ink Image Optimization setting now opens a dialog with a sample
photo processed live through EinkImageProcessor at the selected level,
so users can see the effect of each strength before confirming with
OK or discarding with Cancel.

- new EinkImageSettingItem + EinkImageAdjustmentDialog (plain Dialog;
  M2 AlertDialog title/text slots clamp tall content)
- preview drawable in drawable-nodpi (360x360, 37KB)
- user guide (en, zh-TW): expanded description + before/after
  comparison gallery generated with the same pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
Merge pull request #618 from mjhfunctionalashtanga/feature/volume-double-click-back

Add double-click volume key as Back (opt-in)

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix(gesture): detect volume double-click on key-up, translate new strings

Review fixes for the double-click-for-back gesture:

- Schedule the deferred page turn on key-up instead of key-down. The
  long-press callback only fires at ~400-500ms, after the 250ms window,
  so a turn deferred from key-down always fired mid-hold before the
  long-press/repeat handlers could cancel it: every long-press volume
  adjustment also flipped a page. With key-up scheduling a held key
  never has a pending turn to begin with.
- Track pending page turns per key code so alternating volume keys
  within the window no longer orphans an uncancellable job.
- Fold the duplicated direction logic and the two near-identical
  volume-key handlers into performVolumePageTurn()/handleVolumeKey().
- Mention the dependency on the volume page turn toggle in the setting
  summary, and add the two new strings to all 30 locale files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
Merge pull request #620 from plateaukao/fix/inplace-translate-duplicate-requests

fix(translate): stop in-place translation from requesting each paragraph twice

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix(translate): stop in-place translation from requesting each paragraph twice

Every element visible when in-place translation started was sent to the
LLM API twice: once by the bind-time visibility scan
(maybeRequestTranslation), and ~80ms later by the IntersectionObserver's
initial delivery, whose only guard (data-original-html) isn't set until
a response is applied seconds later. On DOM-churning sites like
news.naver.com, rebinds and rootMargin crossings re-fired pending
elements 3-5x. Both responses were then applied in place - a visible
re-translation on e-ink - and the second apply overwrote the
data-original-html backup with already-translated text.

Instrumented on news.naver.com (proxy around androidApp.getTranslation):
75 calls for 47 elements before; 46/46 initial and 68/68 after five
pages of scrolling - zero duplicates - after the fix.

- Route the IntersectionObserver's in-place branch through
  maybeRequestTranslation so both paths share one guard set
  (data-original-html + text cache + _translateRequested).
- Guard the sibling-mode branch with _translateRequested too; its
  empty-nextElementSibling check has the same in-flight race.
- Reuse the IntersectionObserver across re-injections. Recreating it
  orphaned nodes already in _translateObservedNodes (detached from the
  old observer, skipped by the rebind loop), so off-screen content
  could never translate after a second injection.
- Skip _applyTranslationToElement when data-original-html already
  exists, preserving the original-HTML backup against late duplicates.
- Invoke the JS callback on failed translations (empty string) so the
  in-flight flag clears and the element can be retried; previously a
  failed request blocked its element forever.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
Merge pull request #619 from plateaukao/worktree-autoplay-block

fix: make 'Allow video autoplay' setting actually block feed sites

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix: make 'Allow video autoplay' setting actually block feed sites

The setting only stripped the autoplay attribute and relied on
mediaPlaybackRequiresUserGesture, but Instagram, Facebook, X, and
Threads autoplay muted videos via JS play() from an
IntersectionObserver: muted playback is exempt from the gesture
requirement, and the attribute is never used, so nothing was blocked.

Intercept playback itself instead: patch HTMLMediaElement.prototype.play
so it only succeeds within 1.5s of a trusted click/tap (scrolling never
fires click), reject blocked calls with NotAllowedError exactly like
Chromium's autoplay policy so sites show their own tap-to-play UI, and
pause anything that starts without approval via a capture-phase play
listener. Inject the script with addDocumentStartJavaScript so it runs
before any page script and in every frame, falling back to the old
onPageStarted injection on WebViews without DOCUMENT_START_SCRIPT.
Toggling the setting now also re-applies and reloads the current tab.

Verified on emulator via CDP: programmatic play() rejected, muted
autoplay-attribute video paused, feed videos (shadow DOM included)
never start on load or scroll, and tap-to-play still works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Michael Joel Hall
message:
Add double-click volume key as back button

New opt-in toggle (Settings > Gesture > "Double-click for back",
default off). When enabled, a single volume-key tap still turns the
page but is deferred by a 250ms window so a second tap of the same key
can be caught and mapped to Back instead. Long-press still adjusts
system volume; holding cancels the deferred page turn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01QncYY8gj1nQ3EX5TcGPBTD

author: Daniel Kao
message:
Merge pull request #617 from plateaukao/feat/agent-html-tools

feat(ai): real HTML source, JS eval, web search, and EPUB export for the LLM agent

author: Daniel Kao
message:
feat(ai): give the LLM agent real HTML source, JS eval, web search, and EPUB export

Expand the free-form agent's tool surface so it can act on pages precisely
instead of guessing from truncated reader-mode text:

- read_page_source: download the real served HTML (view-source equivalent)
  via OkHttp with the WebView's cookies + user agent, cached per URL. This is
  the ground truth for writing CSS/DOM selectors.
- Windowed tool results (ToolTextWindow): replace blind 8k truncation with
  offset paging and case-insensitive search-to-element, prefixed with a
  "[chars a..b of N]" header. Applies to read_page_source, read_initial_html,
  read_initial_page, read_current_page.
- run_javascript: eval JS on the live originating tab (test a removal selector
  before persisting it — the user sees it work) or the off-screen WebView
  (extract structured data). 10s timeout, destroyed-tab guarded.
- web_search: query the user's configured search engine off-screen and return
  the result links.
- save_epub: fetch chapter URLs off-screen, extract reader HTML, and write a
  multi-chapter EPUB to Downloads (non-interactive EpubManager.saveEpubDirectly).

Supporting changes: EBWebView.getRawReaderHtml/getRawText are now cancellable
so reader extraction can be bounded by withTimeoutOrNull; EBWebView exposes
isWebViewDestroyed; InitialPageSnapshot carries a WeakReference to the origin
tab. Unit tests cover the windowing logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
refactor: shared ListScaffold + locale-aware base for config activities

Ten config/list activities each re-implemented the same chrome — MyTheme
+ Scaffold + TopAppBar with back arrow and actions — with three
different back-navigation styles and inconsistent icon tinting, and 7
of the 11 Compose activities were missing the app-language
attachBaseContext override, giving users with a non-system UI locale
mixed-language screens.

- LocaleAwareComponentActivity: base applying the locale override; all
  ten activities now extend it (the three per-activity copies of the
  override are deleted).
- ListScaffold(title, onBack, actions, content): shared themed scaffold
  with a standardized onPrimary back arrow; adopted by 7 activities.
  ToolbarConfigActivity (no Scaffold; top bar composed into a custom
  panel layout) and HighlightsActivity (dynamic nav-driven top bar)
  keep their own chrome.
- EmptyListPlaceholder: replaces three copies of the centered
  empty-list box (UserScriptListActivity keeps its intentionally
  different inline style).

Net -209 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix: in-progress edits survive rotation in config screens

None of the Compose config activities declare configChanges, so
rotation recreated them and plain remember state was lost: a user-typed
userscript, an unsaved toolbar/statusbar arrangement, and the settings
search query all vanished.

- SettingActivity: isSearching/searchQuery via rememberSaveable.
- Toolbar/Statusbar config: unsaved arrangements saved as ordinal lists
  with listSaver-based custom savers.
- UserScriptListActivity: the editor dialog state (editing script,
  open flag, source url) and the typed code/url are saveable, with a
  100k-char guard so multi-MB fetched scripts can't overflow the 1MB
  Binder transaction limit during onSaveInstanceState (those remain
  re-fetchable via their sourceUrl). The install-url fetch effect also
  moved from a remember{} side effect to LaunchedEffect and is skipped
  on restore so it can't clobber restored editor state.

Verified on emulator: an unsaved toolbar arrangement (Title added to
the preview) survives a portrait-landscape rotation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix: TwoPaneLayout keeps arrangement and split across orientation; no layout from onMeasure

- initViews() re-derived panel1/panel2 from raw child order on every
  orientation change, silently reverting a user's switchPanels()
  arrangement (while config.translationPanelSwitched still said
  switched) and resetting the dragged split position to 50%. The swap
  is now remembered and re-applied, and the split position is kept
  (defaults still apply on first layout; out-of-range values are
  clamped as before). initDragHandle's cross-axis resets are gone for
  the same reason.
- onMeasure called updatePanels(), which assigns children's
  layoutParams and thus requestLayout() mid-measure, forcing extra
  full-tree layout passes (each one resizing WebViews); it now defers
  via post{}.
- initViews indexed userAddedViews[0]/[1] unguarded behind an empty
  size check; it now returns early when fewer than two panes exist.

Verified on emulator: split screen opens, handle drags to x~322, and
the position survives a horizontal-vertical-horizontal round-trip
(previously reset to center).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Daniel Kao
message:
fix: GptActionDialog no longer wipes typed fields on recomposition

The dialog seeded seven remember { mutableStateOf() } fields and then
unconditionally assigned all of them from the action parameter in the
composable body — a side effect that re-ran on every recomposition of
the outer scope, silently resetting anything the user had typed. The
fields are now initialized once via remember(editActionIndex, action)
and the assignment block is deleted. The window dim-removal hack in the
text slot (an unchecked cast running per recomposition) moved into a
SideEffect with a safe cast.

Verified on emulator: typed action name survives selecting a different
service type (which recomposes the dialog).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
